problem: The ticket proposes extending helix-cli as an alternative walkthrough channel — allowing devs to review AI-generated changes directly in their coding agent (e.g., Claude Code) via the CLI. Currently, helix-cli has no walkthrough or review commands. It supports: login, inspect (db/logs/api/repos), and comments (list/post).

files.why: Main CLI router (57 lines). Switch on command: login, inspect, comments, --version. No walkthrough or review command exists.
Comments subcommand router — the closest existing pattern to a potential walkthrough command. Routes to list.ts and post.ts.
Example of data-fetching CLI command pattern with --ticket flag, --since flag, and JSON output.
Inspect subcommand router — another pattern for a potential walkthrough subcommand.
HTTP client with retry/resilience. Any new walkthrough command would use this to call server APIs.
v1.2.0, Node 18+, CLI binary at dist/index.js. Quality gates: build = tsc, typecheck = tsc --noEmit. No lint or test scripts.

facts: helix-cli has no walkthrough, review, or changes-related commands today
CLI version is 1.2.0 with three top-level commands: login, inspect, comments
The comments command pattern (list/post subcommands, --ticket flag, server API calls) is the closest analog to a potential walkthrough command
HTTP client supports retry with exponential backoff, 30s timeout, and Retry-After handling
The CLI can resolve ticket IDs from environment context (HELIX_TICKET_ID) — established pattern in comments/list.ts
No existing CodeTour file reading or rendering capability in the CLI
The .helix/tickets/*/runs/*/intraview/*.json tour files exist on GitHub branches — a CLI command could fetch and display them
CLI quality gates: npm run build (tsc), npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit). No lint or test scripts.
unknowns: Whether extending the CLI for walkthrough delivery is the chosen direction (ticket is brainstorming, no decision made)
What a CLI walkthrough UX would look like — terminal-based step navigation, piped output to coding agents, or something else
Whether the server API needs new endpoints to support a CLI walkthrough flow or existing /walkthrough endpoints suffice
How a CLI-based walkthrough would integrate with Claude Code or other coding agents — auto-pull mechanism is undefined
